source_rationale: PDGFRA is a well-established marker of adipocyte precursor cells in adipose tissue, marking mesenchymal progenitors committed to the adipogenic lineage
CD34 is expressed on adipose-derived stromal/progenitor cells including adipocyte precursors
DLK1 (Pref-1) is a preadipocyte marker that inhibits adipogenesis and is downregulated upon differentiation
THY1 (CD90) is expressed on adipose progenitor cells and marks uncommitted precursors
ICAM1 marks a subset of adipocyte precursor cells in human adipose tissue
SCA1/LY6A-related genes mark adipose progenitor populations with adipogenic potential
Decorin is highly expressed in adipose stromal/precursor cells as a component of the extracellular matrix
CD19 is a pan-B cell surface marker essential for B cell receptor signaling
MS4A1 (CD20) is a B cell-specific membrane protein widely used as a canonical B cell marker
CD79A is a component of the B cell receptor complex and is specifically expressed in B cells
CD79B pairs with CD79A in the B cell receptor signaling complex and is B cell-specific
PAX5 is a transcription factor essential for B cell identity and commitment
BANK1 is a B cell-specific scaffold protein involved in BCR-induced signaling
BLK is a Src family tyrosine kinase predominantly expressed in B lymphocytes
CD8A expression defines the CD8+ subset of gamma delta T cells
TRDV1 encodes a variable region of the T cell receptor delta chain, marking gamma delta T cells
TRGC1 encodes the constant region of the gamma chain of the T cell receptor in gamma delta T cells
TRDC encodes the constant region of the delta chain, a definitive marker of gamma delta T cells
CD3E is part of the TCR complex and is expressed on all T cells including gamma delta T cells
NKG7 is expressed in cytotoxic lymphocytes including CD8+ gamma delta T cells
Granulysin is expressed in cytotoxic gamma delta T cells with effector function
CD8A is the defining surface marker of CD8+ T cells
CD8B pairs with CD8A to form the CD8 coreceptor on cytotoxic T cells
Granzyme B is a serine protease stored in cytotoxic granules and is a hallmark of cytotoxic function
Granzyme A is a cytotoxic granule protease highly expressed in effector CD8+ T cells
Perforin forms pores in target cell membranes and is essential for cytotoxic T cell killing
NKG7 is highly expressed in cytotoxic lymphocytes and involved in granule exocytosis
Granulysin is an antimicrobial peptide found in cytotoxic granules of effector CD8+ T cells
Interferon gamma is a key effector cytokine produced by activated cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
Fas ligand mediates apoptosis of target cells and is expressed by cytotoxic CD8+ T cells
EOMES is a transcription factor that drives cytotoxic effector programs in CD8+ T cells
PECAM1 (CD31) is a canonical endothelial cell marker involved in cell-cell adhesion at junctions
VE-cadherin (CDH5) is an endothelial-specific adherens junction protein
Von Willebrand factor is produced by endothelial cells and is a classic endothelial marker
KDR (VEGFR2) is the primary VEGF receptor on endothelial cells, essential for angiogenesis
FLT1 (VEGFR1) is a VEGF receptor expressed on endothelial cells
Endoglin (CD105) is a TGF-beta co-receptor highly expressed on endothelial cells
ERG is an ETS family transcription factor that is a master regulator of endothelial gene expression
Claudin-5 is a tight junction protein enriched in endothelial cells
Endomucin is a sialomucin expressed on the luminal surface of endothelial cells
CD56 (NCAM1) is expressed on NK cells, with high expression (CD56bright) characterizing immature NK cells
IL7R is expressed on immature NK cells and NK precursors but downregulated in mature NK cells
KIT (CD117) is expressed on immature NK cell precursors and is downregulated upon maturation
KLRK1 (NKG2D) is an activating receptor expressed early during NK cell development
CD44 is expressed on immature NK cells during their developmental progression
CD62L (SELL) is expressed on immature CD56bright NK cells
Granzyme K is preferentially expressed in immature CD56bright NK cells
XCL1 is a chemokine preferentially produced by immature CD56bright NK cells
CD68 is a pan-macrophage lysosomal glycoprotein marker
IL1B is a pro-inflammatory cytokine highly expressed by inflammatory (M1) macrophages
TNF is a key pro-inflammatory cytokine produced by classically activated macrophages
IL6 is a pro-inflammatory cytokine secreted by inflammatory macrophages
CXCL10 is an interferon-induced chemokine highly expressed in inflammatory macrophages
Inducible nitric oxide synthase is a hallmark of classically activated inflammatory macrophages
CCL2 (MCP-1) is a chemokine produced by inflammatory macrophages to recruit monocytes
CXCL8 (IL-8) is a pro-inflammatory chemokine produced by activated inflammatory macrophages
CD80 is a costimulatory molecule upregulated on M1/inflammatory macrophages
IDO1 is induced by interferon-gamma in inflammatory macrophages
IL7R (CD127) is broadly expressed across all ILC subsets and is a pan-ILC marker
ID2 is a transcription factor essential for the development of all ILC lineages
CD161 (KLRB1) is expressed on most ILC subsets in humans
CD45 (PTPRC) is expressed on all ILCs as hematopoietic cells
KIT (CD117) is expressed on ILC2 and ILC3 subsets
THY1 (CD90) is expressed on ILCs and helps distinguish them from other lymphocytes
T-bet (TBX21) is the master transcription factor defining ILC1 identity
IFN-gamma is the signature effector cytokine produced by ILC1s
IL7R is expressed on ILC1s as part of the common ILC phenotype
IL12RB2 is expressed on ILC1s, enabling responsiveness to IL-12
TNF is produced by ILC1s as part of their type 1 immune response
CD161 (KLRB1) is expressed on human ILC1s
ID2 is required for ILC1 development as a pan-ILC transcription factor
GATA3 is the master transcription factor that defines ILC2 identity and function
IL-13 is a signature type 2 cytokine produced by ILC2s
IL-5 is a key effector cytokine produced by ILC2s that promotes eosinophil activation
IL1RL1 (ST2, IL-33 receptor) is highly expressed on ILC2s and mediates IL-33 responsiveness
CRTH2 (PTGDR2) is a prostaglandin receptor used as a key surface marker for human ILC2s
IL7R is expressed on ILC2s and is important for their survival
KIT (CD117) is expressed on human ILC2s
CD161 is expressed on ILC2s as part of the common ILC surface phenotype
Amphiregulin is produced by ILC2s and contributes to tissue repair
Hematopoietic prostaglandin D synthase is highly expressed in ILC2s
RORγt (RORC) is the master transcription factor defining ILC3 identity
IL-22 is a key effector cytokine produced by ILC3s for mucosal defense
IL-17A is produced by a subset of ILC3s, particularly NCR- ILC3s
KIT (CD117) is highly expressed on ILC3s
NKp44 (NCR2) is expressed on a major subset of human ILC3s (NCR+ ILC3s)
IL7R is expressed on ILC3s and is important for their homeostasis
IL23R is expressed on ILC3s, enabling responsiveness to IL-23 stimulation
Aryl hydrocarbon receptor is expressed in ILC3s and is important for their function
CCR6 is expressed on LTi-like ILC3s and is used to distinguish ILC3 subsets
ID2 is essential for ILC lineage commitment and is expressed in ILC precursors
IL7R is expressed on ILC precursors and is critical for their development
KIT (CD117) is expressed on ILC precursors
TCF7 (TCF1) is expressed in ILC precursors and is important for ILC development
TOX is a transcription factor expressed in ILC precursors required for ILC development
PLZF (ZBTB16) is expressed in ILC precursors that give rise to ILC1, ILC2, and ILC3
NFIL3 is a transcription factor required for early ILC precursor development
TREM2 is a hallmark marker of lipid-associated macrophages in adipose tissue and other contexts
CD9 is highly expressed on lipid-associated macrophages and marks this population
Lysosomal acid lipase is upregulated in lipid-associated macrophages for lipid processing
FABP5 is a fatty acid binding protein highly expressed in lipid-laden macrophages
FABP4 is a lipid chaperone highly expressed in lipid-associated macrophages
Lipoprotein lipase is expressed in lipid-associated macrophages for lipid uptake and metabolism
Osteopontin (SPP1) is highly expressed in lipid-associated macrophages
CD68 is a pan-macrophage marker expressed on lipid-associated macrophages
GPNMB is upregulated in lipid-associated macrophages and involved in lipid metabolism
Galectin-3 is expressed in lipid-associated macrophages and involved in phagocytosis
CD56 is expressed on NK cells, with CD56dim marking the mature cytotoxic subset
CD16 (FCGR3A) is highly expressed on mature CD56dim NK cells and mediates ADCC
Perforin is highly expressed in mature NK cells for cytotoxic function
Granzyme B is abundant in mature NK cell cytotoxic granules
NKG7 is highly expressed in mature NK cells and involved in cytotoxic degranulation
Granulysin is expressed in mature cytotoxic NK cells
CD94 (KLRD1) is expressed on mature NK cells as part of the NKG2 receptor complex
NKp80 (KLRF1) is expressed on mature NK cells
KIR receptors are acquired during NK cell maturation and mark terminally differentiated NK cells
FGFBP2 is highly expressed in mature CD56dim cytotoxic NK cells
TRAV1-2 encodes the invariant TCR alpha chain used by MAIT cells
CD161 (KLRB1) is highly expressed on MAIT cells and is a key surface marker
SLC4A10 is a highly specific marker for MAIT cells in transcriptomic analyses
PLZF (ZBTB16) is a transcription factor expressed in MAIT cells driving their innate-like program
RORγt is expressed in MAIT cells and contributes to IL-17 production capacity
IL18R1 is expressed on MAIT cells enabling cytokine-driven activation
CCR6 is expressed on MAIT cells and facilitates mucosal homing
CD26 (DPP4) is expressed on MAIT cells
CD14 is a canonical myeloid marker expressed on monocytes and macrophages
CD11b (ITGAM) is broadly expressed on myeloid cells including monocytes, macrophages, and granulocytes
CSF1R is expressed on monocytes and macrophages and is essential for myeloid cell survival
Lysozyme is highly expressed in myeloid cells including monocytes and macrophages
S100A8 is a calcium-binding protein highly expressed in myeloid cells
S100A9 forms a heterodimer with S100A8 and is highly expressed in myeloid cells
DAP12 (TYROBP) is an adaptor protein broadly expressed in myeloid cells
FCER1G is an Fc receptor signaling subunit expressed across myeloid lineage cells
CD4 is the defining coreceptor for helper T cells
CCR7 is expressed on naive T cells for homing to secondary lymphoid organs
CD62L (SELL) is expressed on naive T cells for lymph node homing
LEF1 is a transcription factor highly expressed in naive T cells
TCF7 (TCF1) is a transcription factor that maintains naive T cell identity
IL7R (CD127) is expressed on naive CD4+ T cells for homeostatic survival
CD27 is expressed on naive T cells and is downregulated upon terminal differentiation
CD28 is a costimulatory receptor expressed on naive T cells
CD8A is the defining coreceptor for cytotoxic T cells
CD8B pairs with CD8A on conventional CD8+ T cells
CCR7 is expressed on naive T cells for lymph node homing and is a hallmark of the naive state
CD62L (SELL) is expressed on naive CD8+ T cells for lymph node entry
LEF1 is a Wnt pathway transcription factor highly expressed in naive T cells
TCF7 is a transcription factor maintaining naive T cell quiescence and stemness
IL7R is expressed on naive CD8+ T cells for homeostatic proliferation and survival
CD27 is expressed on naive CD8+ T cells
CD56 is expressed on NK-like cells sharing features with natural killer cells
NKG7 is expressed in NK-like cells with cytotoxic potential
CD94 (KLRD1) is expressed on NK-like cells as part of the NK receptor repertoire
CD161 (KLRB1) is expressed on NK-like cells
Granzyme B is expressed in NK-like cells with cytotoxic capacity
Perforin is expressed in NK-like cells for cytolytic function
Some NK-like cells (e.g., NKT cells) may co-express CD3 with NK markers
G-CSF receptor (CSF3R) is highly expressed on neutrophils and their precursors
CD16b (FCGR3B) is a GPI-anchored Fc receptor specifically expressed on neutrophils
S100A8 is highly expressed in neutrophils as part of the calprotectin complex
S100A9 is highly expressed in neutrophils and forms calprotectin with S100A8
CXCR2 is the primary chemokine receptor on neutrophils for IL-8-mediated recruitment
CD66b (CEACAM8) is a specific neutrophil activation marker
MMP9 is stored in neutrophil gelatinase granules and released upon activation
Neutrophil elastase is a serine protease stored in azurophilic granules of neutrophils
Myeloperoxidase is a key enzyme in neutrophil azurophilic granules
Lipocalin-2 (NGAL) is stored in neutrophil specific granules
CD16 (FCGR3A) is the defining marker of nonclassical monocytes (CD14lowCD16+)
CX3CR1 is highly expressed on nonclassical monocytes for vascular patrolling
CDKN1C is a transcriptomic marker highly expressed in nonclassical monocytes
MS4A7 is preferentially expressed in nonclassical monocytes
LILRB2 is expressed on nonclassical monocytes
TCF7L2 is enriched in nonclassical monocytes compared to classical monocytes
LST1 is expressed in nonclassical monocytes
CD163 is a scavenger receptor highly expressed on perivascular macrophages
CD206 (MRC1) is a mannose receptor expressed on perivascular macrophages
LYVE1 is a hyaluronan receptor that marks perivascular macrophages in many tissues
CD68 is a pan-macrophage marker expressed on perivascular macrophages
Coagulation factor XIII A chain is expressed in tissue-resident perivascular macrophages
Collectin-12 is a scavenger receptor expressed on perivascular macrophages
DAB2 is expressed in tissue-resident macrophages including perivascular macrophages
Stabilin-1 is a scavenger receptor expressed on perivascular macrophages
DLK1 (Pref-1) is a canonical preadipocyte marker that is downregulated during adipogenesis
PDGFRA marks mesenchymal progenitors including preadipocytes
CD34 is expressed on preadipocytes in the stromal vascular fraction of adipose tissue
PPARγ begins to be expressed in committed preadipocytes as the master adipogenic transcription factor
C/EBPδ is expressed early in adipogenesis in preadipocytes
Collagen type I is expressed by preadipocytes as mesenchymal/fibroblast-like cells
Lipoprotein lipase expression increases in committed preadipocytes
FABP4 expression begins in preadipocytes committed to adipogenic differentiation
FOXP3 is the master transcription factor defining regulatory T cell identity and function
CD25 (IL2RA) is constitutively highly expressed on regulatory T cells
CTLA4 is constitutively expressed on Tregs and is essential for their suppressive function
Helios (IKZF2) is a transcription factor expressed in thymus-derived regulatory T cells
TIGIT is an inhibitory receptor highly expressed on regulatory T cells
GITR (TNFRSF18) is constitutively expressed on regulatory T cells
OX40 (TNFRSF4) is expressed on activated regulatory T cells
IL-10 is an immunosuppressive cytokine produced by regulatory T cells
CD39 (ENTPD1) is expressed on regulatory T cells and contributes to adenosine-mediated suppression
Alpha smooth muscle actin (ACTA2) is a canonical marker of smooth muscle cells
Smooth muscle myosin heavy chain (MYH11) is a highly specific marker of differentiated smooth muscle cells
Transgelin (SM22α) is expressed in smooth muscle cells and is a well-established marker
Calponin 1 is a smooth muscle-specific contractile protein
Desmin is an intermediate filament protein expressed in smooth muscle cells
Smoothelin is a cytoskeletal protein specific to contractile smooth muscle cells
Myocardin is a master transcriptional coactivator of smooth muscle gene expression
RGS5 is expressed in vascular smooth muscle cells and pericytes
CD56 is expressed on tissue-resident NK cells, often at high levels (CD56bright)
EOMES expression distinguishes tissue-resident NK cells from ILC1s in some tissues
CD49a (ITGA1) is a tissue-residency marker expressed on tissue-resident NK cells
CD103 (ITGAE) is an integrin marking tissue-resident lymphocytes including NK cells
CD69 is a tissue-residency marker expressed on tissue-resident NK cells
CXCR6 is expressed on tissue-resident NK cells, particularly in the liver
KLRC1 (NKG2A) is expressed on tissue-resident NK cells
Hobit (ZNF683) is a transcription factor associated with tissue-resident lymphocyte programs
CLEC9A (DNGR-1) is a C-type lectin receptor specifically expressed on cDC1s
XCR1 is a chemokine receptor specifically expressed on cDC1s
BATF3 is a transcription factor essential for cDC1 development
IRF8 is a transcription factor critical for cDC1 lineage specification
Thrombomodulin (CD141/BDCA-3) is a surface marker of human cDC1s
IDO1 is expressed in cDC1s and involved in cross-presentation and tolerance
CADM1 is a cell adhesion molecule expressed on cDC1s
WDFY4 is required for cross-presentation and is expressed in cDC1s
CD14 is highly expressed on classical (type 1) monocytes
Lysozyme is highly expressed in classical monocytes
S100A8 is highly expressed in classical monocytes
S100A9 is highly expressed in classical monocytes
Versican is expressed in classical monocytes
S100A12 is highly expressed in classical monocytes
CCR2 is highly expressed on classical monocytes for inflammatory recruitment
CD36 is a scavenger receptor expressed on classical monocytes
CD14 is expressed on intermediate monocytes, though at lower levels than classical monocytes
CD16 (FCGR3A) is expressed on intermediate/type 2 monocytes
HLA-DRA is highly expressed on intermediate monocytes which have high antigen-presenting capacity
CSF1R is expressed on intermediate monocytes
CX3CR1 is expressed at intermediate levels on type 2 monocytes
CCR5 is expressed on intermediate monocytes
CD1c (BDCA-1) is a canonical surface marker of cDC2s including the cDC2A subset
CLEC10A (CD301) is expressed on cDC2s
IRF4 is a transcription factor important for cDC2 development and function
FCER1A is expressed on cDC2s in humans
T-bet (TBX21) expression distinguishes cDC2A from cDC2B in recent classifications
MHC class II genes are highly expressed on cDC2A cells
CD11c (ITGAX) is expressed on conventional dendritic cells including cDC2A
CD1c is expressed on cDC2B cells as part of the broader cDC2 population
CLEC10A is expressed on cDC2B cells
IRF4 is expressed in cDC2B cells as a cDC2 lineage transcription factor
RORγt expression has been associated with the cDC2B subset
CD163 expression has been reported on the cDC2B subset distinguishing it from cDC2A
S100A8 is expressed in cDC2B cells, reflecting their more inflammatory profile
S100A9 is expressed in cDC2B cells
CD11c (ITGAX) is expressed on cDC2B cells as a conventional DC marker
FCER1A is expressed on cDC2B cells
